events.text: {"decision":"fail","risk":"high","work_stage":"rough-complete","component_conditions":["installed-defective","non-compliant"],"percent_complete":85,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.9,"event_time":0,"rate":0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["Tape sequence wrong at sill — WRB/flashing tape applied in incorrect lap order at sill pan, compromising shingle-style drainage and creating a water intrusion path at the rough opening","Incorrect sill flashing integration can allow water behind WRB and into wall assembly"],"actions":["Remove and reinstall sill flashing tape in correct shingle-lap sequence per manufacturer instructions and IRC R703: sill pan first, then jamb/head flashing lapping over sill, WRB lapping over flashing","Verify sealant and corner detailing at sill before closing out"],"rationale":"Flashing/WRB integration at the sill must follow shingle-style lapping so water sheds outward and downward; wrong tape sequence reverses laps and creates a water-holding condition, a defective and non-compliant installation requiring rework before close-out.","references":["IRC R703","mfr instructions"]}
